name: encore
description: Encore is the fastest way of developing backend applications

subcommands.name: app
subcommands.description: Commands to create and link Encore apps

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description: Clone an Encore app to your computer

options.description: URL to example code to use

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Link an Encore app with the server

options.description: Force link even if the app is already linked
Auth Key to use for login

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Logs out the currently logged in user

subcommands.name: whoami
subcommands.description: Show the current logged in user

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Checks your application for compile-time errors using Encore's compiler

options.description: Dump generated code (for debugging Encore's code generation)
Start the daemon in the foreground
Environment name to connect to (such as "prod")

subcommands.name: proxy
subcommands.description: Sets up a proxy tunnel to the database

options.description: Environment name to connect to (such as "prod")
Port to listen on (defaults to a random port)

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Resets the databases for the given services. Use --all to reset all databases

options.description: Reset all services in the application

subcommands.name: shell
subcommands.description: Connects to the database via psql shell

options.description: Environment name to connect to (such as "prod")

subcommands.name: eject
subcommands.description: Eject provides ways to eject your application to migrate away from using Encore

subcommands.name: docker
subcommands.description: Docker builds a portable docker image of your Encore application

options.description: Base image to build from
Push image to remote repository

subcommands.name: client
subcommands.description: Generates an API client for your app

options.description: The environment to fetch the API for (defaults to the primary environment)
The language to generate code for ("typescript" and "go" are supported)
The filename to write the generated client code to
Environment name to stream logs from (defaults to the production environment)
Whether to print logs in raw JSON format
Compile for debugging (disables some optimizations)
Address to listen on (for example "0.0.0.0:4000")
Port to listen on
Create a tunnel to your machine for others to test against
Watch for changes and live-reload
To set the secret for development use
To set the secret for production use

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Reports the current version of the encore application

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Checks for an update of encore and, if one is available, runs the appropriate command to update it

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Sets up a secure connection to private environments

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Determines the status of the VPN connection
